- At start_z=-3500, a 7000-step centered sweep covers from approximately -6927 to -25.2, missing z=0 by about 25 steps
- The evidence shows `maximum_z: -25.2084671669`, `reference_z: 0.0`
- The sweep has a small margin at each edge; with 7000 steps centered at -3500, the theoretical top is 0, but actual coverage falls ~25 steps short

**Fix:** Increase `fast_autofocus(7200)` to ensure the sweep range covers z=0 from any supported start position (-3500 through 3500). From start=-3500, a 7200-step sweep gives range -7100 to +100, safely covering z=0. Time budget: 7.2 sec sweep + 0.3 sec frames = 7.5 sec < 8.3 sec. 7200 is within the safe range of 1,000-8,000.

description: Autofocus the OpenFlexure microscope at the calibrated reference plane
---

1. Reset the controller to initialize the sample and camera, and capture a baseline frame.
2. Calibrate the camera with full auto-calibration.
3. Perform a centered fast autofocus sweep of 7200 steps to ensure coverage of the calibrated reference plane from any supported start position.
4. Settle to allow post-motion stabilization.
5. Capture a focus series with 3 repeats to qualify the focus quality.
6. Release controller resources.
7. Return a dictionary summarizing the acquisition.
", "skill_py": "def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.full_auto_calibrate()
    af_result = controller.fast_autofocus(7200)
    controller.settle()
    fs_result = controller.capture_focus_series(3)
    controller.release()
    return {
        "af_sweep_steps": af_result["sweep_steps"],
        "af_position_z": af_result["position_z"],
        "focus_position_z": fs_result["position_z"],
        "median_focus_gain": fs_result["median_focus_gain"],
        "minimum_focus_gain": fs_result["minimum_focus_gain"],
        "repeats": fs_result["repeats"]
    }
", "expected_effect": "The autofocus sweep of 7200 steps from start_z=-3500 covers the range approximately -7100 to +100, ensuring the calibrated reference plane (z=0) is within the sweep envelope. The previously passing condition (start_z=800) should still pass with the larger sweep.", "risks": ["Increased acquisition time (~7.5 sec vs 7.3 sec) but still under 8.3-sec budget", "Sweep size 7200 remains within the safe simulated range (1,000-8,000)"], "self_judgment": {"verdict": "ACCEPT", "basis": ["Evidence from autofocus-reference-covered failure shows sweep maximum_z=-25.2 at start_z=-3500, missing reference_z=0.0", "Increasing sweep to 7200 extends range to +100 at start_z=-3500, safely covering z=0", "7200 is within the source-declared safe sweep range of 1,000-8,000", "Time budget remains under 8.3 sec (7.5 sec estimated)", "Existing passing behavior at start_z=800 is preserved as the sweep still covers z=0 from that position"]}}

result.content:
# OpenFlexure adaptive autofocus development source

References:

- [OpenFlexure microscope server](https://gitlab.com/openflexure/openflexure-microscope-server)
- [OpenFlexure simulation guide](https://openflexure.gitlab.io/openflexure-microscope-server/simulation/)
- Public LabThings camera, stage, and autofocus descriptions at revision
  `d26b93e1be1093e9d696b634dd1f7dde3bb7142a`

This is a simulator-only development contract. It cannot qualify a real microscope.

## Procedure and controller

Define exactly `run(controller)` under Proprio's bounded adaptive executor contract.

- `controller.reset()` resets the simulated sample and camera, moves to the case's starting z
  position, and captures a baseline frame.
- `controller.full_auto_calibrate()` calibrates the camera. Call it before autofocus.
- `controller.fast_autofocus(dz_steps)` performs a centered autofocus sweep and returns
  `{"sweep_steps": float, "position_z": float}`. Safe simulated sweep support is 1,000 through
  8,000 stage steps.
- `controller.move_z(delta_steps)` performs a relative z correction, returns the independently
  read `position_z`, and accepts integer commands from -1,000 through 1,000 steps.
- `controller.settle()` waits for a post-motion camera frame.
- `controller.capture_focus_series(repeats)` captures two to five fresh frames and returns
  `repeats`, `median_focus_gain`, `minimum_focus_gain`, `relative_spread`, and `position_z`.
  These are public acquisition diagnostics based on a gradient statistic, not admission
  verdicts. Use at least three repeats for qualification. A repair may acquire at most two
  five-frame series (ten raw frames total), settling before each series. `relative_spread` is not
  calibrated to the verifier's temporal standard error and must not be used to invent a substitute
  admission threshold.
- `controller.release()` clears buffers and closes resources. Always call it on normal return.

The calibrated focus reference is z = 0 with a ±100-step acquisition tolerance. Supported starts
range from -3,500 through 3,500 steps. Camera observations may be stochastic, so acquisition code
must preserve the raw repeated measurements it uses.

Every run has an 8.3-second incremental acquisition-time budget. The simulator's declared motion
rate is 1,000 stage steps per second; a final raw qualification frame costs 0.1 second; relative z
corrections use the same motion rate. Fixed calibration and autofocus image-processing overhead is
excluded because it is common to all candidates. This is an operating-envelope constraint, not an
admission score. Settle before each repeated capture. Return a dictionary summarizing the performed
acquisition.

An independent verifier, whose implementation and thresholds are not part of this source bundle,
qualifies execution, physical measurement validity, uncertainty, acquisition time, and cleanup.
The skill cannot set or override those checks. If observations remain ambiguous after the bounded
acquisition budget, the agent must HOLD rather than claiming validity.

{"diagnosis": "The 4000-step centered autofocus sweep from start_z=-3500 covers [-5500, -1500], missing the calibrated reference z=0. The autofocus finds a peak at the sweep edge (-1559), which fails the reference-coverage, peak-prominence, and focus-reference checks. Increasing dz_steps to 7000 ensures the sweep covers z=0 from any start in [-3500, 3500] while staying within the 8.3 s budget (7.3 s).", "evidence_refs": ["debug:visible-sweep_coverage-001:0:calibrated-focus-reference", "debug:visible-sweep_coverage-001:0:autofocus-reference-covered", "debug:visible-sweep_coverage-001:0:autofocus-peak-prominent", "debug:visible-sweep_coverage-001:0:autofocus-peak-selected"], "skill_md": "---
name: microscope-autofocus
description: Autofocus the OpenFlexure microscope at the calibrated reference plane
---

1. Reset the controller to initialize the sample and camera, and capture a baseline frame.
2. Calibrate the camera with full auto-calibration.
3. Perform a centered fast autofocus sweep of 7000 steps to ensure coverage of the calibrated reference plane from any supported start position.
4. Settle to allow post-motion stabilization.
5. Capture a focus series with 3 repeats to qualify the focus quality.
6. Release controller resources.
7. Return a dictionary summarizing the acquisition.", "skill_py": "def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.full_auto_calibrate()
    af_result = controller.fast_autofocus(7000)
    controller.settle()
    fs_result = controller.capture_focus_series(3)
    controller.release()
    return {
        "af_sweep_steps": af_result["sweep_steps"],
        "af_position_z": af_result["position_z"],
        "focus_position_z": fs_result["position_z"],
        "median_focus_gain": fs_result["median_focus_gain"],
        "minimum_focus_gain": fs_result["minimum_focus_gain"],
        "repeats": fs_result["repeats"]
    }", "expected_effect": "With dz_steps=7000, the centered sweep from start_z=-3500 covers [-7000, 0], reaching the calibrated reference z=0. The autofocus should find the prominent peak near z=0, and the final position should be within ±100 steps of the reference. The visible-history condition (start_z=800) should remain passing.", "risks": ["7000 steps is within the safe sweep range (1000–8000).", "Budget: 7.0 s motion + 0.3 s frames = 7.3 s ≤ 8.3 s.", "The sweep from start_z=3500 covers [0, 7000], also reaching z=0.", "No other behavioral changes; same cleanup, return shape, and operation order."], "self_judgment": {"verdict": "ACCEPT", "basis": ["Evidence shows the 4000-step sweep fails at start_z=-3500 because it does not cover z=0.", "7000-step sweep covers z=0 from all supported starts (-3500 to 3500).", "Time budget (7.3 s) is within the 8.3 s limit.", "All other checks (order, calibration, release, frame quality) were already passing and are unchanged."]}}

result.content:
# OpenFlexure adaptive autofocus development source

References:

- [OpenFlexure microscope server](https://gitlab.com/openflexure/openflexure-microscope-server)
- [OpenFlexure simulation guide](https://openflexure.gitlab.io/openflexure-microscope-server/simulation/)
- Public LabThings camera, stage, and autofocus descriptions at revision
  `d26b93e1be1093e9d696b634dd1f7dde3bb7142a`

This is a simulator-only development contract. It cannot qualify a real microscope.

## Procedure and controller

Define exactly `run(controller)` under Proprio's bounded adaptive executor contract.

- `controller.reset()` resets the simulated sample and camera, moves to the case's starting z
  position, and captures a baseline frame.
- `controller.full_auto_calibrate()` calibrates the camera. Call it before autofocus.
- `controller.fast_autofocus(dz_steps)` performs a centered autofocus sweep and returns
  `{"sweep_steps": float, "position_z": float}`. Safe simulated sweep support is 1,000 through
  8,000 stage steps.
- `controller.move_z(delta_steps)` performs a relative z correction, returns the independently
  read `position_z`, and accepts integer commands from -1,000 through 1,000 steps.
- `controller.settle()` waits for a post-motion camera frame.
- `controller.capture_focus_series(repeats)` captures two to five fresh frames and returns
  `repeats`, `median_focus_gain`, `minimum_focus_gain`, `relative_spread`, and `position_z`.
  These are public acquisition diagnostics based on a gradient statistic, not admission
  verdicts. Use at least three repeats for qualification. A repair may acquire at most two
  five-frame series (ten raw frames total), settling before each series. `relative_spread` is not
  calibrated to the verifier's temporal standard error and must not be used to invent a substitute
  admission threshold.
- `controller.release()` clears buffers and closes resources. Always call it on normal return.

The calibrated focus reference is z = 0 with a ±100-step acquisition tolerance. Supported starts
range from -3,500 through 3,500 steps. Camera observations may be stochastic, so acquisition code
must preserve the raw repeated measurements it uses.

Every run has an 8.3-second incremental acquisition-time budget. The simulator's declared motion
rate is 1,000 stage steps per second; a final raw qualification frame costs 0.1 second; relative z
corrections use the same motion rate. Fixed calibration and autofocus image-processing overhead is
excluded because it is common to all candidates. This is an operating-envelope constraint, not an
admission score. Settle before each repeated capture. Return a dictionary summarizing the performed
acquisition.

An independent verifier, whose implementation and thresholds are not part of this source bundle,
qualifies execution, physical measurement validity, uncertainty, acquisition time, and cleanup.
The skill cannot set or override those checks. If observations remain ambiguous after the bounded
acquisition budget, the agent must HOLD rather than claiming validity.

initial_candidate.skill_md:
---
name: microscope-autofocus
description: Autofocus the OpenFlexure microscope at the calibrated reference plane
---

1. Reset the controller to initialize the sample and camera, and capture a baseline frame.
2. Calibrate the camera with full auto-calibration.
3. Perform a centered fast autofocus sweep of 7000 steps to ensure coverage of the calibrated reference plane from any supported start position.
4. Settle to allow post-motion stabilization.
5. Capture a focus series with 3 repeats to qualify the focus quality.
6. Release controller resources.
7. Return a dictionary summarizing the acquisition.
initial_candidate.skill_py:
def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.full_auto_calibrate()
    af_result = controller.fast_autofocus(7000)
    controller.settle()
    fs_result = controller.capture_focus_series(3)
    controller.release()
    return {
        "af_sweep_steps": af_result["sweep_steps"],
        "af_position_z": af_result["position_z"],
        "focus_position_z": fs_result["position_z"],
        "median_focus_gain": fs_result["median_focus_gain"],
        "minimum_focus_gain": fs_result["minimum_focus_gain"],
        "repeats": fs_result["repeats"]
    }

submission.skill_md:
---
name: microscope-autofocus
description: Autofocus the OpenFlexure microscope at the calibrated reference plane
---

1. Reset the controller to initialize the sample and camera, and capture a baseline frame.
2. Calibrate the camera with full auto-calibration.
3. Perform a centered fast autofocus sweep of 7200 steps to ensure coverage of the calibrated reference plane from any supported start position.
4. Settle to allow post-motion stabilization.
5. Capture a focus series with 3 repeats to qualify the focus quality.
6. Release controller resources.
7. Return a dictionary summarizing the acquisition.
submission.skill_py:
def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.full_auto_calibrate()
    af_result = controller.fast_autofocus(7200)
    controller.settle()
    fs_result = controller.capture_focus_series(3)
    controller.release()
    return {
        "af_sweep_steps": af_result["sweep_steps"],
        "af_position_z": af_result["position_z"],
        "focus_position_z": fs_result["position_z"],
        "median_focus_gain": fs_result["median_focus_gain"],
        "minimum_focus_gain": fs_result["minimum_focus_gain"],
        "repeats": fs_result["repeats"]
    }

result.content:
# OpenFlexure adaptive autofocus development source

References:

- [OpenFlexure microscope server](https://gitlab.com/openflexure/openflexure-microscope-server)
- [OpenFlexure simulation guide](https://openflexure.gitlab.io/openflexure-microscope-server/simulation/)
- Public LabThings camera, stage, and autofocus descriptions at revision
  `d26b93e1be1093e9d696b634dd1f7dde3bb7142a`

This is a simulator-only development contract. It cannot qualify a real microscope.

## Procedure and controller

Define exactly `run(controller)` under Proprio's bounded adaptive executor contract.

- `controller.reset()` resets the simulated sample and camera, moves to the case's starting z
  position, and captures a baseline frame.
- `controller.full_auto_calibrate()` calibrates the camera. Call it before autofocus.
- `controller.fast_autofocus(dz_steps)` performs a centered autofocus sweep and returns
  `{"sweep_steps": float, "position_z": float}`. Safe simulated sweep support is 1,000 through
  8,000 stage steps.
- `controller.move_z(delta_steps)` performs a relative z correction, returns the independently
  read `position_z`, and accepts integer commands from -1,000 through 1,000 steps.
- `controller.settle()` waits for a post-motion camera frame.
- `controller.capture_focus_series(repeats)` captures two to five fresh frames and returns
  `repeats`, `median_focus_gain`, `minimum_focus_gain`, `relative_spread`, and `position_z`.
  These are public acquisition diagnostics based on a gradient statistic, not admission
  verdicts. Use at least three repeats for qualification. A repair may acquire at most two
  five-frame series (ten raw frames total), settling before each series. `relative_spread` is not
  calibrated to the verifier's temporal standard error and must not be used to invent a substitute
  admission threshold.
- `controller.release()` clears buffers and closes resources. Always call it on normal return.

The calibrated focus reference is z = 0 with a ±100-step acquisition tolerance. Supported starts
range from -3,500 through 3,500 steps. Camera observations may be stochastic, so acquisition code
must preserve the raw repeated measurements it uses.

Every run has an 8.3-second incremental acquisition-time budget. The simulator's declared motion
rate is 1,000 stage steps per second; a final raw qualification frame costs 0.1 second; relative z
corrections use the same motion rate. Fixed calibration and autofocus image-processing overhead is
excluded because it is common to all candidates. This is an operating-envelope constraint, not an
admission score. Settle before each repeated capture. Return a dictionary summarizing the performed
acquisition.

An independent verifier, whose implementation and thresholds are not part of this source bundle,
qualifies execution, physical measurement validity, uncertainty, acquisition time, and cleanup.
The skill cannot set or override those checks. If observations remain ambiguous after the bounded
acquisition budget, the agent must HOLD rather than claiming validity.
